failedAssertions.reason: Authentication barrier on /api/daemon-event endpoint - requires daemon token auth via getDaemonTokenAuthContextOrNull. Full E2E flow test could not complete due to missing valid daemon credentials.

frictions.description: Browser automation (agent-browser) experienced timeouts when navigating to localhost:3000 - likely due to existing Chrome processes or resource constraints
frictions.resolution: Pivoted to code analysis approach - reviewed implementation at source code level, which provided thorough verification of broadcast deduplication logic

frictions.description: Authentication barrier on /api/daemon-event endpoint prevents full E2E testing
frictions.resolution: None - requires valid daemon token for authentication

appliedUpdates.description: Created service definitions for PostgreSQL, Redis, www, and broadcast services with healthchecks and start/stop commands
Created user testing documentation with URLs, service order, concurrency limits, flow validator guidance for agent-browser and curl

flowReports.summary: Daemon terminal events correctly trigger broadcast with delivery-loop refetch. Code verified at daemon-event route (line 1427-1455), broadcast schema includes 'delivery-loop', and cache invalidation is properly implemented. 76 tests passed.

No spam on unchanged state verified through three-layer protection: (1) Backend only broadcasts when didUpdateTerminalDispatchStatus is true, (2) Process-effects only broadcasts when effects are drained, (3) React Query deduplicates identical refetch requests.
End-to-end latency test blocked by authentication requirement. The /api/daemon-event endpoint returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized, requiring daemon token authentication via getDaemonTokenAuthContextOrNull.
flowReports.blocker: Authentication barrier - requires valid daemon credentials
